data.title: Sierra Bancorp declares $0.25 dividend, up to 1M share repurchase authorization
data.description:
• Sierra Bancorp (BSRR) declared $0.25/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.76%
  • Payable Nov. 14; for shareholders of record Nov. 3; ex-div Nov. 3.
  • Additionally, the company said its board has authorized a share repurchase of up to 1M shares of common stock.
  • The new stock buyback program commences after the current share repurchase program expires on October 31, 2025, and continues until October 31, 2026. 
  • No shares remain available for repurchase under the current share repurchase program.
  • See BSRR Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: First Hawaiian GAAP EPS of $0.59 beats by $0.07, revenue of $226.39M beats by $8.06M
data.description:
• First Hawaiian press release (FHB): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.59 beats by $0.07.

data.title: Watch hospital stocks as HCA, Community Health post Q3 beats
data.description:
Hospital stocks are expected to trade higher on Friday after HCA Healthcare (NYSE:HCA), the number one for-profit healthcare provider in the U.S., and its peer Community Health Systems (NYSE:CYH) reported better-than-expected financials with their latest quarterly results.

Community Health Systems (NYSE:CYH) reported its Q3 2025 financials on Thursday after the close, exceeding Street forecasts for both revenue and earnings while raising its full-year outlook to a level above consensus.

HCA Healthcare (NYSE:HCA) added ~4% on Friday in the premarket, as its Q3 2025 financials also stood above expectations, and its revised earnings guidance for this year beat the consensus.

Their peers, such as Tenet Healthcare (THC) and Universal Health Services (UHS), are currently trading higher in the premarket after the announcements. Other hospital stocks to watch include Surgery Partners (SGRY), SunLink Health Systems (SHY), Select Medical Holdings (SEM), Acadia Healthcare (ACHC), and LifeStance Health Group (LFST).

For Q3, HCA reported roughly $19.2B in revenue, exceeding the consensus by $600M with ~10% YoY growth as its same facility revenue per equivalent admission grew ~6% YoY.

Meanwhile, HCA’s same-facility admissions and same-facility emergency room visits rose ~2% YoY and ~1%, respectively, and its same-facility inpatient and outpatient surgeries jumped ~1% YoY each, while the company reported ~50.6K licensed beds at the end of the quarter.  

Its adjusted quarterly EBITDA rose ~19% YoY to $3.9B, while adj. EBITDA margins improved to ~20% from ~19% in the year-ago period. The company reported $6.96 of non-GAAP earnings per share with ~42% YoY growth, exceeding the consensus by $1.92.

Looking ahead, HCA raised its full-year outlook for revenue and diluted EPS to $75.0B - $76.5B and $27.00 - $28.00 from $25.50 - $27.00 and $74.0B - $76.0B in the prior guidance and compared to $75.1B and $26.35 projected by analysts, respectively.

data.title: Flagstar Bank Q3 earnings benefit from lower expenses, growth in C&I loans
data.description:
Flagstar Bank N.A. (NYSE:FLG) slightly adjusted the low end of its 2025 earnings guidance as it reduced expectations for noninterest income. Meanwhile, Q3 earnings met the Wall Street consensus, helped by lower expenses and provision for credit losses.

The most recent quarter's results exhibited "strong growth in C&I (commercial and industrial) lending, a higher net interest margin, and well controlled operating expenses, while our problem loans continued to decrease, and we further reduced our commercial real estate exposure," Chairman, President, and CEO Joseph M. Otting said.

The company now expects full-year 2025 adjusted loss per share of $0.36-$0.41, compared with the consensus estimate of -$0.41 and its prior guidance for a loss of $0.35-$0.40.

Full-year 2025 noninterest income guidance was cut to $310M-$320M from its prior outlook of $320M-$360M. Net interest income is still expected to be $1.70B-$1.75B vs. the Visible Alpha consensus of $1.72B.

The company reaffirmed guidance for adjusted EPS of $0.75-$0.80 in 2026 and $2.10-$2.20 in 2027.

Flagstar Bank (NYSE:FLG) stock rose 0.6% in premarket trading.

Q3 adjusted EPS of -$0.07, matching the average analyst estimate, narrowed/widened from -$0.14 in Q2 and -$0.69 in last year's Q3.

Total revenue of $519M, beating the $517M consensus, rose from $496M in the prior quarter and fell from $623M in the year-ago period.

Net interest income of $425M, trailing the Visible Alpha estimate of $434M, increased from $419M in Q2 and dropped from $510M in Q3 2024.

Total adjusted noninterest income dropped to $73M from $77M in the prior quarter and $136M in the year-ago quarter.

Q3 adjusted pretax preprovision net revenue climbed to $15M from $9M in the previous quarter and -$43M a year ago.

Provision for credit losses of $38M, vs. the Visible Alpha estimate of $59M, declined from $64M in Q2 and $242M in last year's Q3.

Adjusted operating expenses of $457M fell from $460M in the previous quarter and $652M a year ago.

Total loans and leases held for investment dipped to $62.7B at Sept. 30, 2025, from $64.1B at June 30. Deposits of $69.2B declined from $69.7B at the end of Q2.

Total commercial and industrial loans rose 3.1% Q/Q to $14.9M, led by growth in specialized industries and regional commercial and corporate banking, which climbed 28% Q/Q to $5.14B.

data.title: Putin responds to new U.S. sanctions against Rosneft and Lukoil
data.description:
Crude oil is taking a breather after soaring by the most in four months, with WTI futures (CL1:COM) now resting firmly above $60 per barrel. The catalyst for the jump was new U.S. sanctions on two of Russia's biggest energy giants: Rosneft and Lukoil. The two behemoths have been helping to "raise revenue for the Kremlin's war machine," but have been largely untouched by the West since the war in Ukraine started in February 2022.

Thought bubble: Frustrated over how long negotiations are taking between Kyiv and Moscow, President Trump has been seeking additional leverage to force concessions and both sides to the table. The next measures were said to be a choice between supplying Ukraine with Tomahawk missiles or strengthening sanctions, but ultimately the latter won out as a more predictable choice. It's being taken now as there is plenty of oil awash in the market, so the restrictions will have less economic fallout. Gas prices in the U.S. fall below $3 a gallon on average following OPEC+ production increases

While their effectiveness will come down to enforcement, the Trump administration is now threatening a financial blackout with secondary sanctions. It's a big deal as many foreign banks conducting oil transactions clear and settle in the U.S. under the petrodollar system. Financial institutions can now be forced to cease dealing with Rosneft, Lukoil, and their dozens of subsidiaries, or risk being cut off from the American banking system and their ability to deal in dollars.

"The new U.S. sanctions are serious for us and will have certain consequences, but they will not significantly impact our economic well-being," Russia's Vladimir Putin told reporters. "It's an attempt to put pressure on Russia. No self-respecting country ever does anything under pressure. Russia is a self-respecting country."

Will it work? Things will definitely get more complicated for buyers of Russian crude, like Indian and Chinese refiners, though a shadow fleet of hundreds of tankers has been operating for years despite hurdles in payments and insurance. There have also been transactions completed in other currencies, like Chinese yuan or the UAE's dirhams, but those have been limited as they lack the necessary liquidity and often involve a dollar conversion at some point. Further discounting may also be necessary to take on the additional risk, such as the falsification of documents, as well as the layering and laundering of oil trades through non-sanctioned intermediaries.

data.title: FG Nexus commences options trading of stock on NYSE
data.description:
FG Nexus (NASDAQ:FGNX) said on Friday that its common stock is available for options trading on the NYSE Arca Options and NYSE American Options.

Shares were +1.08% pre-market to $3.75.

FG Nexus options trade under the ticker FGNX.

"We expect the options listing to improve share liquidity and provide investors greater flexibility for risk management and position strategies," said the company in a statement.

The New York Stock Exchange is a unit of Intercontinental Exchange (ICE).

data.title: Procter & Gamble in charts: Modest Y/Y sales gain seen across all segments in FQ1
Earnings Snapshot: General Dynamics tops Q3 estimates, secures $19.3 billion in orders companywide
European indexes flat as investors read economic indicators
data.description:
Update: The revised post reflects market movements as of 7.10am ET.

London (UKX) -0.09% to 9,589. UK's retail sales rise 1.5% Y/Y in September. UK car production tumbled 27% Y/Y in September. The GfK Consumer Confidence Index for the UK rose to -17 in October 2025 from -19 in September, beating expectations for a decline to -20 as shoppers geared up for the Black Friday sales.UK PMIs rise in October as manufacturing and services show signs of recovery.

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.09% to 24,226, Germany’s manufacturing, services, and composite PMIs rise in October.

France (CAC:IND) -0.55% to 8,217, Business activity in France falls at quickest pace since February. Consumer confidence in France rose to 90 in October.

Euro Area PMIs strengthen in October as manufacturing and services activity expands.

In other parts of Europe, Spain's unemployment rate rises more than expected in Q3.

The manufacturing confidence indicator in Denmark rose to 92.3 in October.

Producer prices in Sweden rose 0.5% Y/Y in September. Producer prices in Finland fell by 0.3% Y/Y in September.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) turned 0.75% higher to 568.8. 

Coming up in the session: Euro Area and UK's flash PMI readings expected shortly.

In the bond market, the yield on the US 10-year Treasury was up 2 basis points to 4.01%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 4 basis points to 2.62%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 1 basis point to 4.43%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)
